sanskrit: सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति। अजानता महिमानं तवेदं मया प्रमादात् प्रणयेन वापि॥ यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु। एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम्॥
transliteration: sakheti matvā prasabhaṃ yad uktaṃ he kṛṣṇa he yādava he sakheti / ajānatā mahimānaṃ tavedaṃ mayā pramādāt praṇayena vāpi // yac cāvahāsārtham asatkṛto ’si vihāra-śayyāsana-bhojaneṣu / eko ’thavāpy acyuta tat-samakṣaṃ tat kṣāmaye tvām aham aprameyam //
literal_meaning: Thinking (You) as 'friend,' whatever I rashly said—'O Kṛṣṇa, O Yādava, O friend'—not knowing this greatness of Yours, through carelessness or affection; and whatever disrespect I showed in play, on bed, seat, or at meals, alone or before others—O infallible One, immeasurable One, I ask Your forgiveness for all that.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences acute cognitive dissonance: the same person he treated as an equal buddy is revealed as the cosmic Absolute. He retroactively scans every casual gesture, nickname, and shared sandwich for implicit disrespect, now amplified by awe. The verse is a self-repair ritual: naming the micro-violations to restore relational equilibrium.

shadow: fear of abandonment by the friend

psychological_state: Post-revelation relational recalibration: shifting from horizontal friendship to vertical reverence while preserving attachment security.
trigger_scenario: You discover your easy-going roommate is actually the CEO who signs your paycheck. You mentally replay every time you borrowed their hoodie without asking.
anchor_text: Seeing the divine in a friend dissolves every careless word into humble apology.
